mnemonics: Epsilon-Greedy: Explore Eagerly, Exploit Excellently. Use Epsilon to Explore, Greed to Grab Gains.
UCB: Upper Confidence Bound = Use Certainty for Betting. Pick arms with high Upper Confidence for a sure Bet.
Regret: Remember Every Good Reward Avoids Grief Eventually. Total Regret is the cost of not always Taking The Really Excellent Arm.
keyPoints: The Exploration-Exploitation Dilemma is the core challenge: balancing trying new arms (exploration) with choosing the best-known arm (exploitation).
Regret is the key performance metric, defined as the difference between the reward of the optimal arm and the reward of the chosen arm over time.
Different algorithms (ε-Greedy, UCB, Gradient Bandit) solve the dilemma with different strategies for assigning value to and selecting actions.
commonMistakes: Always using a fixed ε (epsilon) value. It often needs to decrease over time to shift from exploration to exploitation.
Confusing the reward distribution with the action-value estimate. The estimate is a prediction; the distribution is the true, underlying probability.
Ignoring the non-stationary nature of a problem. If reward distributions change over time, algorithms must forget old information (e.g., using a constant step-size).
